message: Operation 'createShortUrl' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is confusing with unclear use cases and poor structure. Reorganized to clearly explain the findIfExists behavior, input parameters, decision logic, and return value. Simplified language for better AI agent interpretability while preserving important behavioral details.

data.current_description:
Creates a new short URL.

**Param findIfExists**: This new param allows to force shlink to return existing short URLs when found based on provided params, instead of creating a new one. However, it might add complexity and have unexpected outputs. These are the use cases: * Only the long URL is provided: It will return the newest match or create a new short URL if none is found. * Long url and custom slug are provided: It will return the short URL when both params match, return an error when the slug is in use for another long URL, or create a new short URL otherwise. * Any of the above but including other params (tags, validSince, validUntil, maxVisits): It will behave the same as the previous two cases, but it will try to exactly match existing results using all the params. If any of them does not match, it will try to create a new short URL.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new short URL or returns an existing one based on the findIfExists parameter. Accepts a long URL and optional parameters including custom slug, tags, expiration dates (validSince/validUntil), and visit limits (maxVisits). When findIfExists is true, returns an existing short URL if all provided parameters match exactly; otherwise creates a new one. When findIfExists is false (default), always creates a new short URL. Returns the created or matched short URL object with its short code and metadata. Requires API key authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description is confusing with unclear use cases and poor structure. Reorganized to clearly explain the findIfExists behavior, input parameters, decision logic, and return value. Simplified language for better AI agent interpretability while preserving important behavioral details.

data.current_description:
Creates a new short URL.

**Param findIfExists**: This new param allows to force shlink to return existing short URLs when found based on provided params, instead of creating a new one. However, it might add complexity and have unexpected outputs. These are the use cases: * Only the long URL is provided: It will return the newest match or create a new short URL if none is found. * Long url and custom slug are provided: It will return the short URL when both params match, return an error when the slug is in use for another long URL, or create a new short URL otherwise. * Any of the above but including other params (tags, validSince, validUntil, maxVisits): It will behave the same as the previous two cases, but it will try to exactly match existing results using all the params. If any of them does not match, it will try to create a new short URL.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new short URL or returns an existing one based on the findIfExists parameter. Accepts a long URL and optional parameters including custom slug, tags, expiration dates (validSince/validUntil), and visit limits (maxVisits). When findIfExists is true, returns an existing short URL if all provided parameters match exactly; otherwise creates a new one. When findIfExists is false (default), always creates a new short URL. Returns the created or matched short URL object with its short code and metadata. Requires API key authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description is confusing with unclear use cases and poor structure. Reorganized to clearly explain the findIfExists behavior, input parameters, decision logic, and return value. Simplified language for better AI agent interpretability while preserving important behavioral details.
